description_long: This object is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by two prominent dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood appears to have a natural, medium-brown finish, with visible grain and a faceted, polygonal surface texture. The metal hoops are dark, likely iron or steel, and provide structural reinforcement to the barrel. The top and bottom are open, suggesting the barrel is currently empty and does not have a lid. There are no visible handles, spouts, or decorative markings on the exterior. The overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for storage or transport of goods in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, open at both ends and lacking handles or markings. The surface is faceted and reinforced, giving it a sturdy, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, wood grain, and both metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Angled view with more pronounced polygonal facets and partial visibility of the barrel's interior.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the wood and metal hoops clearly visible and a slightly different lighting angle.
description_view_3: Angled view with strong polygonal patterning and partial interior visibility, similar to view 1 but from the opposite side.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
